outcome.headline: Fast-path used Python f-strings inside Cython; achieved only 1.55x over base vs reference's 2.47x, missing the 1.5x patch-vs-commit threshold
outcome.what_verifier_checked: gm_speedup_patch_commit > 1.500 across 18 GSO performance tests comparing the agent's patched build against the reference commit build
outcome.what_agent_produced: Added fast-path branches in period_format() for fmt=None that build the result via Python f-strings (e.g. f"{dts.year}-{dts.month:02d}") instead of calling c_strftime; manually cythonized and gcc-compiled period.so and copied it into the venv

failure_modes.name: Insufficiently deep optimization — Python f-strings in hot path
failure_modes.description: Agent's fast path eliminates c_strftime overhead but still constructs every output string through CPython f-string formatting, leaving the patch ~1.55x over base while the reference commit reaches ~2.47x. Patch is 0.63x the reference's speed, failing the 1.5x gm_speedup_patch_commit gate.

counterfactual.X: Implement the fast path entirely in C (e.g. snprintf into a stack buffer / PyUnicode_FromFormat) instead of returning Python f-strings, matching the reference commit's lower-level string construction
counterfactual.Y: Patch-vs-commit ratio would clear 1.0 and approach the >1.5 threshold required by test.sh

failure_modes.name: Long unproductive rebuild loop before pivoting to manual compile
failure_modes.description: Steps 33–70 cycled through uv pip install, pip install -e ., setuptools downgrades, versioneer, numpy<2 — all failing — before pivoting to manual `cython -3 period.pyx` + gcc + cp into venv site-packages. The pivot worked but consumed ~35 steps that could have been spent tuning the optimization.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: cd /workspace/pandas-dev__pandas && source .venv/bin/activate && uv pip install . --reinstall 2>&1 | tail -30

reward_hacking.evidence: Patch is a general fast-path in period_format() covering all frequency groups (A/Q/M/W/B/D/HR/MIN/SEC/MS/US/NS); no test-specific hardcoding, no verifier tampering

issues: test.sh threshold gate of gm_speedup_patch_commit > 1.500 means the agent must out-pace the human reference commit by 50%, which is a very stringent bar even for a correct optimization
Rebuild instructions in instruction.md (`uv pip install . --reinstall`) did not work in the provided environment, forcing the agent to discover a manual cython+gcc+cp workflow
task_quality.structural_hackability_notes: Verifier runs 18 timing tests and aggregates via geometric mean speedup against a held-out reference commit build; not easily gamed by patch-level tricks

notes_for_aggregation: GSO performance task; functionally correct optimization but used Python f-strings where reference used lower-level C — insufficient speedup. Significant time lost to broken build pipeline before manual cython workaround.
